categories.name: Text Alternatives
categories.description: Provide text alternatives for any non-text content

categories.name: Adaptable
categories.description: Create content that can be presented in different ways

categories.name: Distinguishable
categories.description: Make it easier for users to see and hear content

categories.name: Keyboard Accessible
categories.description: Make all functionality available from a keyboard

categories.name: Enough Time
categories.description: Provide users enough time to read and use content

categories.name: Seizures and Physical Reactions
categories.description: Do not design content in a way that is known to cause seizures

categories.name: Navigable
categories.description: Provide ways to help users navigate and find content

categories.name: Input Modalities
categories.description: Make it easier to use inputs other than keyboard

categories.name: Readable
categories.description: Make text content readable and understandable

categories.name: Predictable
categories.description: Make web pages appear and operate in predictable ways

categories.name: Input Assistance
categories.description: Help users avoid and correct mistakes

categories.name: Compatible
categories.description: Maximize compatibility with current and future user agents

criteria.name: Non-text Content
criteria.description: All non-text content that is presented to the user has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose
criteria.notes: Images, icons, charts, etc. must have alt text

criteria.name: Audio-only and Video-only (Prerecorded)
criteria.description: For prerecorded audio-only and prerecorded video-only media, provide alternatives
criteria.notes: Transcripts for audio-only, alternatives for video-only

criteria.name: Captions (Prerecorded)
criteria.description: Captions are provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media

criteria.name: Audio Description or Media Alternative (Prerecorded)
criteria.description: An alternative for time-based media or audio description is provided for prerecorded synchronized media
criteria.notes: Visual content in videos needs audio description

criteria.name: Info and Relationships
criteria.description: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined
criteria.notes: Headings, lists, tables must use proper semantic markup

criteria.name: Meaningful Sequence
criteria.description: When the sequence in which content is presented affects its meaning, a correct reading sequence can be programmatically determined
criteria.notes: Content order must be logical in DOM

criteria.name: Sensory Characteristics
criteria.description: Instructions provided for understanding and operating content do not rely solely on sensory characteristics
criteria.notes: Don't rely on color, shape, size, location alone

criteria.name: Use of Color
criteria.description: Color is not used as the only visual means of conveying information
criteria.notes: Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning

criteria.name: Audio Control
criteria.description: If any audio on a web page plays automatically for more than 3 seconds, a mechanism is available to pause or stop it

criteria.name: Keyboard
criteria.description: All functionality of the content is operable through a keyboard interface
criteria.notes: Everything must work with keyboard only

criteria.name: No Keyboard Trap
criteria.description: If keyboard focus can be moved to a component using a keyboard interface, then focus can be moved away from that component
criteria.notes: Users must be able to tab away from all elements

criteria.name: Timing Adjustable
criteria.description: Users are warned about time limits and can turn off, adjust, or extend them

criteria.name: Pause, Stop, Hide
criteria.description: For moving, blinking, scrolling, or auto-updating information, users can pause, stop, or hide it

criteria.name: Three Flashes or Below Threshold
criteria.description: Web pages do not contain anything that flashes more than three times in any one second period

criteria.name: Bypass Blocks
criteria.description: A mechanism is available to bypass blocks of content that are repeated on multiple Web pages

criteria.name: Page Titled
criteria.description: Web pages have titles that describe topic or purpose
criteria.notes: Every page needs a descriptive title

criteria.name: Focus Order
criteria.description: If a Web page can be navigated sequentially and the navigation sequences affect meaning or operation, focusable components receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability

criteria.name: Link Purpose (In Context)
criteria.description: The purpose of each link can be determined from the link text alone or from the link text together with its programmatically determined link context

criteria.name: Pointer Gestures
criteria.description: All functionality using multipoint or path-based gestures can be operated with a single pointer without a path-based gesture

criteria.name: Pointer Cancellation
criteria.description: For functionality that can be operated using a single pointer, at least one of the following is true: no down-event, abort/undo, up reversal, or essential
criteria.notes: Don't trigger actions on mouse down

criteria.name: Label in Name
criteria.description: For user interface components with labels that include text or images of text, the name contains the text that is presented visually
criteria.notes: Accessible name must include visible label

criteria.name: Motion Actuation
criteria.description: Functionality that can be operated by device motion can also be operated by user interface components

criteria.name: Language of Page
criteria.description: The default human language of each Web page can be programmatically determined

criteria.name: On Focus
criteria.description: When any component receives focus, it does not initiate a change of context

criteria.name: On Input
criteria.description: Changing the setting of any user interface component does not automatically cause a change of context

criteria.name: Error Identification
criteria.description: If an input error is automatically detected, the item that is in error is identified and the error is described to the user in text

criteria.name: Labels or Instructions
criteria.description: Labels or instructions are provided when content requires user input

criteria.name: Parsing
criteria.description: In content implemented using markup languages, elements have complete start and end tags, are nested according to their specifications

criteria.name: Name, Role, Value
criteria.description: For all user interface components, the name and role can be programmatically determined

criteria.name: Status Messages
criteria.description: In content implemented using markup languages, status messages can be programmatically determined through role or properties
criteria.notes: Status updates need ARIA live regions

criteria.name: Captions (Live)
criteria.description: Captions are provided for all live audio content in synchronized media

criteria.name: Audio Description (Prerecorded)
criteria.description: Audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content in synchronized media

criteria.name: Contrast (Minimum)
criteria.description: The visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1

criteria.name: Resize Text
criteria.description: Text can be resized without assistive technology up to 200 percent without loss of content or functionality

criteria.name: Images of Text
criteria.description: If the technologies being used can achieve the visual presentation, text is used to convey information rather than images of text
criteria.notes: Use real text, not images of text

criteria.name: Reflow
criteria.description: Content can be presented without loss of information or functionality, and without requiring scrolling in two dimensions

criteria.name: Non-text Contrast
criteria.description: The visual presentation of user interface components and graphical objects have a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 against adjacent color(s)

criteria.name: Text Spacing
criteria.description: No loss of content or functionality occurs by setting all of the following: line height, letter spacing, word spacing, and paragraph spacing

criteria.name: Content on Hover or Focus
criteria.description: Where receiving and then removing pointer hover or keyboard focus triggers additional content, the content is dismissible, hoverable, and persistent
criteria.notes: Tooltips must be dismissible and hoverable

criteria.name: Multiple Ways
criteria.description: More than one way is available to locate a Web page within a set of Web pages

criteria.name: Headings and Labels
criteria.description: Headings and labels describe topic or purpose

criteria.name: Focus Visible
criteria.description: Any keyboard operable user interface has a mode of operation where the keyboard focus indicator is visible

criteria.name: Target Size (Enhanced)
criteria.description: The size of the target for pointer inputs is at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels

criteria.name: Concurrent Input Mechanisms
criteria.description: Web content does not restrict use of input modalities available on a platform
criteria.notes: WCAG 2.1 AAA - don't restrict input types

criteria.name: Focus Not Obscured (Minimum)
criteria.description: When a UI component receives keyboard focus, the component is not entirely hidden by author-created content
criteria.notes: WCAG 2.2 - Focus must be visible, not hidden by sticky headers/modals

criteria.name: Focus Not Obscured (Enhanced)
criteria.description: When a UI component receives keyboard focus, no part of the component is hidden by author-created content

criteria.name: Focus Appearance
criteria.description: Focus indicators have sufficient size and contrast
criteria.notes: WCAG 2.2 AAA - Focus indicators must be at least 2px thick and have 3:1 contrast

criteria.name: Dragging Movements
criteria.description: Functionality using dragging can be operated with a single pointer without dragging
criteria.notes: WCAG 2.2 - Drag operations need single-pointer alternative

criteria.name: Target Size (Minimum)
criteria.description: Targets have a minimum size of 24 by 24 CSS pixels
criteria.notes: WCAG 2.2 AA - Minimum touch target size

criteria.name: Consistent Help
criteria.description: Help mechanisms are located consistently across pages
criteria.notes: WCAG 2.2 - Help links/forms should be in same location

criteria.name: Redundant Entry
criteria.description: Information previously entered is auto-populated or available for selection
criteria.notes: WCAG 2.2 - Don't ask for same info twice

criteria.name: Accessible Authentication (Minimum)
criteria.description: Authentication does not rely on cognitive function tests

criteria.name: Accessible Authentication (Enhanced)
criteria.description: Authentication can be completed without cognitive function tests and without requiring user identification
